1823.name: Bedroom
1823.description: The stench of demonkind is nearly overpowering here. A large, stone basin filled with gelatinous slime occupies the center of the room, large gas bubbles occasionally breaking the oozing surface to release a foul odor. A couple of waste-fouled, wooden perches stand off to one side, their bases set firmly into the stone.

npcs.name: Seductive succubus
npcs.description: creation can affect your loins.  Long muscular legs end in taloned feet, while her supple back is adorned with velvet-furred batwings caressing her

1797.name: Outside the Iron Gate
1797.description: You stand outside the gates of a dark, forbidding city. Strange cries and shrieks can be heard from beyond the gate; occasionally a burst of maniacal laughter. Clouds of thick vapor hover far overhead within the massive underground cavern, drifting above the city.

npcs.name: Masindaar daemon
npcs.description: the flexing of its broad ebon wings.  The rough, stone surface of its body pulses softly as webs of amber light flow across it.

1798.name: Inside Hedros City
1798.description: Strange forms move along the streets and every patch of greater darkness seems to conceal furtive movement. Many of the creatures you see have baleful red eyes, though some are milky white, others a burnished silver. A large iron gate looms to the west; the city proper is to the east.

npcs.name: Newt
npcs.description: on a pear-shaped body.  A pretty stupid looking little critter.

1799.name: Iron Street
1799.description: You have come before a small shop displaying a black banner with a hammer and shield in silver. The iron gates of the city are still visible to the west and Iron Street continues east. Another road leads off to the south.

1800.name: The Hammered Shield
1800.description: The sweet smell of oiled steel quickens your pulse as you examine the array of displayed armors. Most are for sale, but some like the ancient suit of Bandwar mail, are obvious showpieces.

npcs.name: Porx
npcs.description: Porx's jowly, porcine features jiggle slightly as he turns his head from side to side as if seeking a new perspective.

1801.name: Practice Yard
1801.description: Practice dummies stand rigidly at attention, their wooden forms hacked and splintered by sword and axe cuts. The black gravel yard is fenced in, offering doorways only to the east and west.

npcs.name: Klak
npcs.description: shoulders and arms.  He really seems to know the art of combat; the skill he demonstrates indicates that he would be a very tough opponent.

1802.name: A House of Arms
1802.description: A long counter runs the length of the room, swords, axes and hammers carefully arranged along its length. Various other weapons hang from racks on the walls or lean against the wall.

npcs.name: Xomax
npcs.description: better end of any trade he makes.  His thin, bird-like frame looks fragile but he is surprisingly strong.

1803.name: Iron Street
1803.description: A door beckons to the north. Glancing in, you see the silver gleam of steel; a weapon shop. Iron Street goes west from here, there is a bustling square to the east.

npcs.name: Guardian
npcs.description: demon's build is broad and powerful and he looks none too friendly.

1804.name: Commerce Square
1804.description: The square is filled with the bustle of people about their business. They look like people - more or less. They do have a definite demonic cast to their features and a pronounced atmosphere of pure malice about them.

1805.name: Temple Plaza
1805.description: You stand in a small plaza before the Dark Temple. The buildings nearby are all tastefully decorated in glossy black and blood red. A larger square is to the south, a sign reading 'Krakkzub's Inn' hangs over a door to the east.

1806.name: Tasty Snacks Eatery
1806.description: You find yourself in a charming tavern which boasts small wooden tables covered with red and white checkered table-cloths. The delightful aroma of broiled meat fills the air and the clatter of crockery makes you think of home.

npcs.name: Kkrakzub
npcs.description: snout of a pig.  Handsome if you go in for that sort of thing, repellent if you don't.  He smells of stagnant water and mildew.

1808.name: Street of Gold
1808.description: It may be called the Street of gold but you don't see any richness in evidence. The pavement of the street throws back little glints but they are more of a ruddy crimson color. A bustling square is plainly visible to the west and the street continues east.

1809.name: Street of Gold
1809.description: In the distance to the north you can see a squat building of sturdy construction which fronts on a small square which is directly to your north. The sluggish waters of River Hedros are to your south, the Street of Gold continues to the east and west.

npcs.name: Guardian
npcs.description: demon's build is broad and powerful and he looks none too friendly.

1810.name: Square of the Crimson Coin
1810.description: The large, blocky structure to the north bears a sign which proclaims the building to be the First Bank of Hedros. Since you haven't seen any other banks in evidence, you conclude that demons just naturally like to put on airs.

1811.name: First Bank of Hedros
1811.description: Definitely a good strong bank. Thick walls, iron bars and locked doors abound. A constant stream of customers come and go while you watch; a lot of money moves through this bank. It would take a smart banker to keep track of it all.

npcs.name: Eniak
npcs.description: ruthlessness.  His sharp eyes search you keenly, apparently assessing your

1812.name: Street of Gold
1812.description: Large bronze gates, green with ages of tarnish, bulk in the distance to the east. To the south a narrow, rickety bridge spans River Hedros, creaking and groaning dangerously as people move across it. The Street of Gold continues to the west.

1813.name: Inside the Bronze Gates
1813.description: You stand inside a set of large, bronze gates. The gates are not the mellow color of new bronze but green with age. A large street heads off to the west.

1814.name: Footbridge
1814.description: This bridge looks almost as if it was built as an afterthought to the rest of the city. Because it is so narrow only one person can cross it at a time. It sways sickenly as you cross, threatening to pitch you off into the dark water below.

1815.name: Basalt Road
1815.description: Flat black, the road seems to absorb all light, reflecting none. The feeling is of walking across an invisible surface which holds you suspended above a infinite void. A small bridge is to the north and the road continues east.

1816.name: Before Porgus House
1816.description: You stand at the foot of the squat, black tower known as Porgus House. The tower is roughly circular and rises perhaps 50 feet over your head. The only break in the smooth, ebon surface is a large, rectangular window near the apex of the tower.

1819.name: Tower Top
1819.description: The roof is fairly small, only about 15 feet in diameter. It is composed of the same black stone that makes up the rest of the tower and slants steeply from its high center toward the sheer edge.

npcs.name: Huge, guardian demon
npcs.description: past conflicts but his opponents probably look alot worse.

1821.name: Stairway Switchback
1821.description: You have come to a fairly large landing where the stairs reverse the angle of their descent. A large stone door regulates passage to the east and the stairs continue toward the top and bottom of the tower.

1822.name: Study
1822.description: A somewhat conventional looking office with a desk, some pictures and a rug on the floor.

1824.name: Tower Entrance
1824.description: Kinda cramped in here. Since the tower looks much larger from the outside, you can only assume that there are other rooms behind the stone wall to the east. A massive, stone slab with a large handle on it stands to the west. A set of stairs climbs up into the tower.

1825.name: Alley at Levee
1825.description: You move along a narrow alley running from the edge of the river on the south, to a large street to the north.

1826.name: Levee
1826.description: The oily, black water flows by this small spit of land, occasionally sending small wavelets up to lap about your feet. You can enter the river to the south or enter the small alley which looms to the north.

1827.name: On the Dark River
1827.description: You rock slowly with the current of the river, which is fairly slow and smooth at this point. Directly north of you is the Levee where you can exit the river and get back on dry land. The river continues east and west.

1828.name: The Foul River
1828.description: A rotten smell comes strongly off the river at this point seeming to get stronger to the south. Looking that direction, you see a narrow arm of rock extending out over that edge of the river. A jagged barrier of rock prevents travel in that direction, which is probably just as well given the stench that comes from there.

1829.name: Beneath a Narrow Bridge
1829.description: You are crossing beneath a narrow footbridge which seems to be very shabbily constructed. It is so narrow and rickety that it sways sickeningly whenever an occasional passerby lurches across its fragile span.

1830.name: The Roiling Waters
1830.description: As the waters pass into the city proper, they churn roughly as though protesting the path they have been given to travel. The waters seem calm enough to the east and west, but you can hardly help notice the change in the character of the water slightly to the west. The river becomes darker and more viscous in that direction.

1831.name: East of the City
1831.description: You are just east of a dark and menacing city. Winged, reptilian shapes flap noisily through the murky heights of this dreary subterranean world. To the east, the river cuts around a rock formation and is gone from sight.

1846.name: Iron Street
1846.description: As you look south you see the sluggish flow of the Hedros River. Right next to you, on the north, is a high wall from beyond which you can hear the sound of combat and occasionally an incongruous bark of laughter. Iron street continues to the east and west.

1847.name: Iron Bridge
1847.description: Made entirely of black iron, this bridge looks like it would support even the heaviest of freight wagons. You have a good view of the dark waters of the river as they extend into the distance in the east and west but the revolting stench rising from the viscous flow makes you want to move along.

1848.name: Night Street
1848.description: You stand on the west end of Night Street. Braxis lane heads off to the south and to your north you see a large, iron bridge. A vague, sulfurous smell wafts out from the shop to the west. Night Street continues to the east.

1849.name: The Azure Flame
1849.description: A number of tables spotted around the room display low cost amulets and good- luck charms but the good stuff is behind the counter where they can be easily watched. A set of marble stairs leads up to an unknown destination above.

npcs.name: Shazzy
npcs.description: Shazzy look like a thin Santa Claus.  He mutters to himself as he putters

1851.name: Night Street
1851.description: You stand in the center of a dark road which stretches off to the east and west. The banks of the Hedros river, steep and dangerous, block travel to the north. The smell of leather and dry goods is bourne to you by a soft breeze from the south.

1852.name: Herbix`s Emporium
1852.description: You see a lot of things that could be called essential miscellanea. Bags, torches, lanterns and a hoard of other such items line the shelves or sit in small floor displays. Everything is clean and organized as Herbix is a fastidious businessman.

npcs.name: Herbix
npcs.description: the shelves and muttering GP, PMAs and VOLUME.

1853.name: Night Street
1853.description: The sluggish, black waters of the Hedros River move slowly from east to west, blocking travel to the north. Cries of pain and misery come to you from the east where a grim town-square beckons. The doors of a small shop are open immediately to the south.

1855.name: Gertina`s Fine Housewares
1855.description: This is a truly beautiful room. The furniture tastefully arranged, the room's appointments placed with loving care and an eye for color and balance. The room seems to say, "Lay down, rest here, enjoy me. " However, you notice small "keep off" signs on all the furniture and "don't touch" signs on all the decorations.

npcs.name: Gertina
npcs.description: mild-mannered but has a tongue sharp enough to flay your back.

1856.name: Misery Square
1856.description: Soft cries and whimpers fill the air with a soft song of misery. The wretches who have been crucified, hung from stakes, or broken over racks in this hellish square must have been deemed too physically infirm to make good slaves to the Hedros demons, before or after death.

1857.name: Street of Sin
1857.description: Soft cries and moans of anguish come from the west like a soft song of misery. Boisterous talk and an occasional bark of laughter come through a small door to the south. There is a sign above the door featuring an arm, its hand severed and hanging by a scrap of flesh. The name "Lefty's" has been stenciled across the front of the building in bold, red letters.

npcs.name: Guardian
npcs.description: demon's build is broad and powerful and he looks none too friendly.

npcs.name: Lefty
npcs.description: is always dealing with volitile tempers.  His left hand is missing and the stump has been adorned with a tatoo of a large and bloodshot eye.

1859.name: Flechzzt`s Flesh Mart
1859.description: A brothel. No question about it. Everything done up in tacky red velvet and gold brocade. Truly icky. A hallway leads off to the south, the door to Lefty's is to the east.

1860.name: Plush Hallway
1860.description: Hmmm, there is a certain odor...not unpleasant...it smells a little of sweat. As if someone had been exercising recently. Doors lead off to the east and west. The hall continues to the south, the entry to Flechzzt's is to the north.

1861.name: The Blue Room
1861.description: A mighty small room. Just enough room to rest, sleep or whatever. Everything is decorated entirely in blue.

1862.name: The Green Room
1862.description: A mighty small room, just room enough to rest, sleep or whatever. Everything is decorated entirely in green.

1863.name: Plush Hallway
1863.description: You stand at the southern end of the hall. There are doors to the east and west.

1864.name: The Red Room
1864.description: A mighty small room. Just room enough to rest, sleep or whatever. Everything is decorated entirely in red.

1866.name: Slave Market
1866.description: Misery is thick in the atmosphere of this dingy place. A large auction block and a small table are the only appointments. A heavy iron door is to the south, the Street of Sin is to the north.

npcs.name: Troxel
npcs.description: He stinks of sweat and misery.

1807.name: The Comfy Cot
1807.description: Small doorways are spaced evenly about the walls of this large room, each of them offering a safe place for you to sleep. A large desk stands in the center of the room.

npcs.name: Quottzox
npcs.description: as if he wanted a bite.  As a licensed innkeeper he is bonded though so you will probably be as safe here as anywhere.

1817.name: Tower Wall
1817.description: You cling to the wall of a small tower, perched atop a few small footholds jutting from its smooth surface. You can continue to climb up, or descend to the tower's base.

1818.name: Near the Window
1818.description: You cling to the wall alongside a large, open window. Whatever is beyond the window is shrouded in darkness.

1820.name: Landing
1820.description: You stand before a large window in a very small room. A heavy, steel grate is used to block access to a narrow set of stairs leading down. If you are nimble enough, you could climb out the window and cling to the face of the tower. The room is barren except for a pile of badly smashed bones on the floor, near the grate.

1850.name: Mage`s Lab
1850.description: Shelves against the north wall contain many oddments of wood and metal. Small beakers and vials, containing oddly colored liquids and fine powders are neatly racked on a counter against the south wall. A large bookcase of books is to the west. Wide marble stairs lead down to the magic shop.

npcs.name: Pralindria
npcs.description: Smoldering eyes peer icily at you from beneath a mane of raven black hair.  Her perfect face is that of irony, for the hate and malice in it isn't
